name: Azure Security Posture Snapshot
description: Snapshot a subscription's Microsoft Defender for Cloud security posture: pull the overall secure score (azure_security_secure_score) and the active assessments (azure_security_list_assessments) in parallel via the live openagentic_azure MCP. The analyze transform classifies each finding by severity, sorts unhealthy findings descending (High → Medium → Low), takes the top 10, pre-computes the rows_html, and folds an empty-banner when zero unhealthy assessments are returned. The platform LLM writes a 3-4 bullet operator-facing narrative on the top posture concerns ordered by severity, CoT preamble is stripped, and a webhook_response renders the final HTML report. Empty-subscription case (no Defender plan, or a fully clean posture — common for small subs) renders a 'No unhealthy assessments' banner instead of an empty table. Note: a 401/403 from Azure (caller lacks Defender Reader / Security Reader) surfaces as a hard node_error from the oap-azure-mcp error_response wrapper — same engine-level UX as every other MCP-driven template.

meta.purpose: Snapshot an Azure subscription's Microsoft Defender for Cloud security posture: secure score plus the top 10 unhealthy assessments ranked by severity, with an LLM narrative on what to fix first.
how_it_works: 1. Fans the trigger out to two parallel openagentic_azure MCP calls: azure_security_secure_score and azure_security_list_assessments.
2. A merge converges both branches keyed by source.
3. A single analyze transform classifies each assessment by severity, sorts unhealthy findings (High → Medium → Low), keeps the top 10, and pre-renders rows_html.
4. The platform LLM writes a 3-4 bullet operator narrative on the top posture concerns ordered by severity.
5. A CoT-stripper transform removes any LLM preamble.
6. webhook_response renders the HTML posture snapshot. Clean-posture short-circuit: when no unhealthy assessments exist, a 'No unhealthy assessments' banner renders instead of an empty table.
meta.expected_output: HTML posture snapshot with: secure score, count of unhealthy assessments by severity, top 10 finding rows, and a 3-4 bullet LLM 'what to fix first' narrative.
meta.useful_when: Monthly security review, pre-audit posture check, after a high-CVSS CVE drop, or when a customer/internal CISO asks 'what's our Defender posture this week?'.

operations.value: {{ (() => { const b = input; const get = (o) => { if (!o || typeof o !== 'object') return null; if (typeof o.subscription_id === 'string' && o.subscription_id) return o.subscription_id; if (typeof o.content === 'string') { try { const p = JSON.parse(o.content); if (p && typeof p.subscription_id === 'string') return p.subscription_id; } catch (e) {} } return null; }; return get(b && b.secure_score) || get(b && b.assessments) || 'unknown'; })() }}

{{ (() => { const branch = input && input.secure_score; if (!branch || typeof branch !== 'object') return null; let payload = branch; if (typeof branch.content === 'string') { try { payload = JSON.parse(branch.content); } catch (e) { payload = null; } } if (!payload || !Array.isArray(payload.secure_scores)) return null; const ascScore = payload.secure_scores.find(s => (s && (s.name === 'ascScore' || (s.display_name || '').toLowerCase().includes('overall')))); const pick = ascScore || payload.secure_scores[0]; if (!pick) return null; const p = (typeof pick.percentage === 'number') ? pick.percentage : null; if (p === null) return null; return Math.round(p * 1000) / 10; })() }}

{{ (() => { const branch = input && input.assessments; if (!branch || typeof branch !== 'object') return []; let payload = branch; if (typeof branch.content === 'string') { try { payload = JSON.parse(branch.content); } catch (e) { payload = null; } } if (!payload || !Array.isArray(payload.assessments)) return []; const sevOrder = { High: 0, Medium: 1, Low: 2, Informational: 3 }; const unhealthy = payload.assessments.filter(a => { if (!a || typeof a !== 'object') return false; const status = String(a.status || '').toLowerCase(); return status !== 'healthy' && status !== ''; }); const copy = unhealthy.map(a => { const sev = String(a && a.severity || 'Low'); const rank = sevOrder[sev] !== undefined ? sevOrder[sev] : 4; return Object.assign({}, a, { __sev_rank: rank }); }); copy.sort((x, y) => { const xr = (typeof x.__sev_rank === 'number') ? x.__sev_rank : 4; const yr = (typeof y.__sev_rank === 'number') ? y.__sev_rank : 4; return xr - yr; }); return copy; })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.assessments_array) ? input.assessments_array : []; return a.slice(0, 10); })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.assessments_array) ? input.assessments_array : []; return a.length; })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.assessments_array) ? input.assessments_array : []; const out = { High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0, Other: 0 }; for (const f of a) { const s = String((f && f.severity) || 'Other'); if (out[s] !== undefined) out[s] += 1; else out.Other += 1; } return out; })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.top_findings) ? input.top_findings : []; if (a.length === 0) return 'No unhealthy security assessments found.'; const esc = s => String(s == null ? '' : s).replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); return a.map(f => { const sev = esc((f && f.severity) || 'Unknown'); const name = esc((f && (f.display_name || f.name)) || ''); const status = esc((f && f.status) || ''); const desc = esc(((f && f.description) || '')).slice(0, 240); return `${sev}${name}${status}${desc}`; }).join(''); })() }}

{{ (() => { const c = (typeof input.finding_count === 'number') ? input.finding_count : 0; if (c === 0) return '

No unhealthy security assessments. Microsoft Defender for Cloud reports zero unhealthy assessments for this subscription. This usually means the subscription is small, recently remediated, or Defender is not yet enrolled on its resource types.

'; return ''; })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.top_findings) ? input.top_findings : []; const top = a.slice(0, 5).map(f => ({ severity: (f && f.severity) || 'Unknown', display_name: (f && (f.display_name || f.name)) || '', status: (f && f.status) || '', description: ((f && f.description) || '').slice(0, 240), categories: (f && f.categories) || null })); return { finding_count: (typeof input.finding_count === 'number') ? input.finding_count : 0, severity_summary: input.severity_summary || { High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0, Other: 0 }, secure_score_pct: (typeof input.secure_score_pct === 'number') ? input.secure_score_pct : null, top_findings: top }; })() }}

data.label: Security Posture Narrative
data.prompt:
You are a security operator writing a 3-4 bullet operator-facing narrative on the Azure security posture for subscription {{steps.analyze.subscription_label}}.

Secure score: {{steps.analyze.secure_score_pct}}%
Unhealthy assessments: {{steps.analyze.finding_count}} (High: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.High}}, Medium: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.Medium}}, Low: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.Low}})
Top findings (JSON):
{{steps.analyze.summary_payload}}

Output rules (MUST follow):
1. Start the response with a single `-` character — no preamble, no "Here is", no "The user wants", no thinking out loud.
2. 3-4 short bullets, each on its own line, each beginning with `-`.
3. Bullet 1: the single highest-severity finding — call out the display_name, the severity, and a concrete one-sentence remediation step.
4. Bullet 2: the second-highest concern with the same structure.
5. Bullet 3: a roll-up of the remaining Medium / Low findings — combined count + one-line theme (e.g. 'three more storage public-network-access findings').
6. Bullet 4 (optional): a posture observation (the secure-score number plus whether it improved or regressed if trend data were available — for now just contextualize the current value, e.g. 'A 62% secure score leaves significant headroom for remediation').
7. If finding_count is 0, emit one bullet stating no unhealthy assessments were found and one bullet recommending a Defender enrollment check.

data.label: HTML Security Posture Report
data.bodyTemplate:
Azure Security Posture Snapshot — subscription {{steps.analyze.subscription_label}}

Secure score: {{steps.analyze.secure_score_pct}}% · Unhealthy assessments: {{steps.analyze.finding_count}} · High: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.High}} · Medium: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.Medium}} · Low: {{steps.analyze.severity_summary.Low}}

{{steps.analyze.empty_banner}}

Operator Narrative

{{steps.clean_narrative.clean_content}}

Top Findings

{{steps.analyze.rows_html}}
SeverityFindingStatusDescription
